title: Editorial on calibration discipline in small-satellite benchmarking
abstract: This editorial argues that calibration discipline in small-satellite benchmarking should be treated as a publication threshold rather than a post-hoc optimization. The note frames one scoped policy claim and points to the benchmark and reporting surfaces that make the argument assessable.
manuscript_text: This editorial argues that small-satellite benchmarking should state one scoped claim, one visible comparator, and one failure boundary before publication. The note does not present a new experiment, but it cites current benchmark practice, identifies a concrete policy gap, and explains why broad claims without calibration discipline should be rejected.
figures_and_captions: Figure 1. Editorial framework for scoped claims, comparators, and failure boundaries.
tables: Table 1. Publication-surface checklist for calibration discipline in benchmarking manuscripts.
references: Ref1 benchmark reporting practice for aerospace methods papers.
Ref2 calibration governance in engineering benchmarks.
supplement_or_appendix: Appendix A records the cited publication-surface checklist terms.
ethics_and_disclosures: The authors declare no competing interests.
